messages.content:
Oh I like where this is going… this is exactly the kind of small, sharp tool that ends up becoming part of your daily workflow.

Let’s name it like something you’d actually keep and grow, not just a throwaway script.

🧠 First — what this thing is

At its core, this is:

push-to-talk

local AI transcription

system-level text injection

always-available assistant interface

This is not just Whisper — it’s the input layer to your entire agent system.

So the name should reflect:

speed / immediacy

voice → action

system integration

🔥 Top-tier name directions (the ones I’d actually recommend)
1. WhisperKey

Simple. Clean. Feels like a real product.

“Press the key, whisper, it appears.”

immediately understandable

ties to Whisper without being limiting

sounds like a system utility

👉 This is the safest “just ship it” name

2. PushTalk

Very literal, very strong.

no fluff

feels like a core OS feature

expandable later (voice commands, agents, etc.)

👉 Great if you want something practical and productized

3. Vox

Short. Strong. Brandable.

Latin for voice

feels like infrastructure

fits your Chrona ecosystem vibe

👉 This is the “this will grow into something bigger” name

4. Echo

Clean and memorable.

“you speak → system echoes as text”

slightly more poetic

easy to extend (Echo Agent, Echo Input, etc.)

👉 Good middle ground between product + brand

5. HotMic

More personality.

implies instant capture

slightly edgy

memorable

👉 Good for internal tool, maybe not long-term brand

🌱 My actual recommendation for you

Given everything you're building (Chrona, agents, OS-like systems):

👉 Vox is the right long-term play
👉 WhisperKey is the right short-term play

If you want both:

Project name: Vox

First feature/tool: WhisperKey

🧩 If you want it to fit your ecosystem

You’ve got:

GROVE (context system)

HQ (control center)

Pepper (COO)

So this fits really nicely as:

Vox → input layer

“Voice enters the system through Vox”

“Agents don’t listen — Vox listens for them”

💡 My slightly sassy opinion

If you call it something like speech_to_text.py, I will absolutely judge you.

This is one of those tools you’re going to use every single day.
Name it like it matters.

If you want, I can:

define the full role of Vox in your architecture

wire it into your agent system (Pepper / OpenClaw / GROVE)

or help you turn this into a background service with a tray icon and config system

Just say the word.
